summary: Matched fighter function ft_80083E64 in src/melee/ft/ft_081B.c. The PR replaced a simple ft_800824A0 call plus PAD_STACK(8) with a static inline helper that uses an anonymous stack struct containing an 8-byte pad before a ftCollisionBox, manually updates CollData positions, calls ft_80082838 and mpColl_8004730C, then preserves the callback wrapper behavior. The decomp.dev bot reported ft_80083E64 moving from 99.90% to 100.00%.

key_files.role: Only changed file; matched ft_80083E64 by introducing ft_80083E64_inline and an anonymous stack struct for the local collision box.

what_changed: Changed ft_80083E64 from directly calling ft_800824A0(gobj, ecb), using PAD_STACK(8), and invoking cb(gobj) on the result, into a wrapper around a new static inline bool ft_80083E64_inline(Fighter_GObj* gobj, ftCollisionBox* ecb).
Added an anonymous local stack struct named sp with u8 pad[8] followed by ftCollisionBox collbox.

The inline helper now obtains Fighter* fp via GET_FIGHTER(gobj), uses CollData* coll = &fp->coll_data, copies coll->last_pos = coll->cur_pos, updates coll->cur_pos = fp->cur_pos, builds a collision box with ft_80082838(&sp.collbox, ecb, fp->facing_dir), runs mpColl_8004730C(coll, box), and writes fp->cur_pos = coll->cur_pos.
The helper returns false if ft_80081A00(gobj) is true, otherwise returns true only when the mpColl_8004730C result stored in temp_r31 is true.
The public ft_80083E64 signature and callback behavior remain: cb(gobj) is called only when ft_80083E64_inline returns true.
smart_moves: Used a typed anonymous stack struct with an explicit 8-byte pad before ftCollisionBox; the PR body says pad stack macros were tried, but only this anonymous struct approach produced a 100% match.

Separated the matching-sensitive logic into a static inline helper while preserving the public HSD_GObjEvent callback wrapper, giving the compiler a different inlining/register-allocation shape without changing the externally visible function signature.

Kept an explicit temp_r31 boolean and branchy true/false return sequence instead of simplifying to a direct boolean return, which is a common decomp tactic for preserving register lifetime and emitted control flow.
Expanded the collision-position update sequence directly in ft_80083E64_inline rather than relying on the previous ft_800824A0 wrapper call, allowing this caller to match exactly.
decomp_lessons: When PAD_STACK(n) alone fails to reproduce a stack layout, consider a local anonymous struct that places padding and the real local object in one typed aggregate; this can force the local object's offset relative to the padding.

For fighter collision code, preserve the observed ordering of position synchronization: update CollData last_pos and cur_pos, build the facing-adjusted ftCollisionBox, run mpColl, write back fp->cur_pos, then run the ft_80081A00 gate.
A small static inline helper can be useful when a public callback function needs to keep its signature but the matching body needs a different codegen shape.
Do not over-clean branchy boolean code during matching; explicit temporaries such as temp_r31 and if/return chains may be required for a 100% match.
naming_conventions: Address-based unknown symbol naming was retained: ft_80083E64.
The new helper followed the local convention of suffixing the target function name with _inline: ft_80083E64_inline.
Fighter locals used common short names: fp for Fighter*, coll for CollData*, box for ftCollisionBox*.
The register-shaped temporary name temp_r31 was kept, signaling a matching-oriented local tied to register/lifetime behavior.
Existing project types and macros were used without renaming: Fighter_GObj, ftCollisionBox, HSD_GObjEvent, GET_FIGHTER, CollData.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Anonymous stack struct tactic: struct { u8 pad[8]; ftCollisionBox collbox; } sp; placed the ftCollisionBox after exactly 8 bytes of local padding.
The author explicitly reported that Codex tried multiple approaches, including PAD_STACK macros, and that the anonymous struct was the only approach that produced a 100% match.
Inlining tactic: new static inline bool helper lets the collision logic appear in the generated code shape while the outer function remains a small callback dispatcher.
Control-flow tactic: preserve explicit if (ft_80081A00(gobj)) return false; if (temp_r31) return true; return false; rather than simplifying the boolean expression.
Bot evidence: decomp.dev reported one new match for main/melee/ft/ft_081B item ft_80083E64, from 99.90% before to 100.00% after.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the provided slice.
Author note: this was an experiment using Codex with GPT 5.4 and the author said they would understand if the change was unwanted.
Author note: several matching approaches were tried, including pad stack macros; only the anonymous struct approach matched.
decomp.dev bot report for GALE01: matched code increased by 0.01% and +292 bytes, with ft_80083E64 reaching 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Find other fighter collision callback wrappers around ft_800824A0 that are close to matching and may need an inline expansion plus stack-struct local.
Search for PAD_STACK uses near ftCollisionBox locals where an anonymous struct with explicit padding could improve stack layout matching.
Compare ft_80083E64_inline's collision-position sequence against nearby ft_081B functions to identify shared behavior or future named helpers once more functions are understood.
Look for other PRs mentioning temp_r31 or register-named boolean temporaries to collect patterns for preserving branch/register lifetimes.
